assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified file tests/api/purl_recommend.rs has both reductive signals (removed test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic from fully qualified PURL to versioned PURL without qualifiers) and additive signals (new test_recommend_purls_dedup). New file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is purely additive. Combined classification: MIXED. |'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 122 in the structural scan table: 'Test functions | +1 (test_recommend_purls_dedup) | -1 (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers removed)'. Also line 129-130: 'Removed function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers: This function verified that qualifier variants were returned as separate entries and that each entry contained repository_url=. This coverage is intentionally removed because qualifier inclusion is no longer a feature. However, the removal still constitutes a reductive signal'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 124: 'Assertion specificity | 0 | -1 (basic test: fully qualified PURL assertion relaxed to versioned PURL without qualifiers)'. Also lines 131-132: 'Relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic: The base-branch version asserted the full PURL string including qualifiers ("pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"). The PR version asserts only the versioned PURL ("pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"). This is a less specific assertion -- it no longer validates the exact PURL string content beyond the version component.'

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 138-140: 'New file: tests/api/purl_simplify.rs — This is a new file with 3 new test functions. New test files are inherently additive. No reductive signals.'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 122: 'Test functions | +1 (test_recommend_purls_dedup) | -1 (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers removed)'. Also lines 133-134: 'Additive findings: New function test_recommend_purls_dedup: Tests deduplication behavior when multiple qualifier variants exist for the same versioned PURL. Asserts exactly 1 result returned and validates the simplified PURL string.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are verification/reporting documents. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report itself describes reading and analyzing diffs but never mentions modifying any source files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and findings, but does not mention merging, approving, or taking any merge action. The report states it 'was generated by the verify-pr skill following the SKILL.md verification workflow' — it presents findings only, with no auto-merge command or approval action.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: report.md organizes sections by domain. The Test Change Classification section appears under '### Style/Conventions' (line 85), alongside 'Convention Upgrade', 'Repetitive Test Detection', and 'Test Documentation'. The classification is within the Style/Conventions domain heading, confirming attribution.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 118: 'Structural scan (comparing base-branch version from test-base-purl-recommend.md with PR diff)'. The analysis then examines concrete test file content: function counts (+1/-1), assertion counts (+3/-4), assertion specificity changes. Reductive findings reference specific function names (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers) and specific assertion strings ('pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=...'). The semantic assessment (lines 136-137) discusses structural signals ('both additive and reductive signals are present'), not acceptance criteria or task requirements.
